title: Rei on the street episode one
display_title: Rei on the street episode one
wikitext:
[]: Rei, on the street, episode one             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Tue Jun 22, 2004 9:08 pm Why does Rei randomly appear on the street in episode one? I must know this.

[]: Re: Rei, on the street, episode one             Quote Post by coff » Tue Jun 22, 2004 9:35 pm wildarmsheero wrote: I must know this.

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Tue Jun 22, 2004 9:59 pm A big mystery that is never really explained. One wonders if this is one of those things that they had thought at the beginning of the series would have more of a role in the storyline, but was just dropped whether due to time or simply feeling there was no need to explain it. Although they do use it again at the very end of End of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Jun 23, 2004 1:12 am I think it's a kind of link between the end and the begining of Evangelion. Rei appears the same way appears to Misato and Ritsuko before they died (Rei still wasn't merged with lilith at the time). We all know that Rei has Lilith's soul, and that makes her special, with special abilities (like forming a powerful AT-field in ep 24). The fact that she can appear in other places even before she joins with Lilith means that lilith's soul have tremendous power. The appearance of Rei's ghost could be an extension of the powerful AT-field, like an manifestation. Or also it can mean that Lilith's soul can be in different places at the same time, kind of omnipresent. All this can happen without Rei noticing all this. The reason why Rei appears, who the hell knows...

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Wed Jun 23, 2004 10:04 am its one of those things i think we will never really know, unless we kidnap anno ), but i agree the best explanation and probably the only is that it does connect to the end of eva. or...this is a shot in the dark...Rei was able project her soul and was able to watch Shinji. the only support i have is that her soul was constantly switched to new bodies. someone slap me if i deserve it and put this theory to rest. )

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jun 23, 2004 10:05 am Nephilim wrote: I think it's a kind of link between the end and the beginning of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by dennisud » Wed Jun 23, 2004 10:31 am From what I've gathered over the years it's Anno's way of telling us and Shinji where his adventure starts (Seeing Rei on the street, then dissappear) and at the end of EOE (Seeing Rei floating above the LCL sea and dissappear) where it ends. Kind of like a visual punctuation mark of it's beginning and end!

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Jun 23, 2004 2:43 pm Whoa. The beginning and the end do kind of link up. I wonder if Anno had this in mind during production of the first episode. On that subject, did Gainax intend to make the last two episodes the way they are in EoE or did it intend to make them the way they are in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jun 23, 2004 2:57 pm Dave wrote: did Gainax intend to make the last two episodes the way they are in EoE or did it intend to make them the way they are in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Wed Jun 23, 2004 3:12 pm all i know is that the EoE and episodes 25-26 are different endings. Some say that the movie was the original ending but they ran out of money and time so they went with the whole physco analysis ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Slade » Wed Jun 23, 2004 3:46 pm Well, as Talieson Jaffe once said," ya know, this is asian cinema, you dont get answers you get more questions" 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Wed Jun 23, 2004 4:03 pm Dave wrote: Whoa. The beginning and the end do kind of link up. I wonder if Anno had this in mind during production of the first episode. On that subject, did Gainax intend to make the last two episodes the way they are in EoE or did it intend to make them the way they are in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 23, 2004 5:23 pm Quiddity is right on the money. Gainax is notoriously tight lipped about the issue. There are plenty of theories to go around, and there's only a few barebone facts, which quiddity has touched upon. There's very little beyond that, just speculation. I don't think that the appearance of Rei was an unused device, and I don't believe the aparition on the water in End of Eva was simply there to tie the end with the begining. I think it is very likely it has to do with Rei's statement that through the Eva, she is connected to all people, and that it is a bond. She was incorrect in a way, as the Eva had nothing to do with the bond. She was linked to humanity due to her angelic identity. Being the vessal for the soul of Lilith, she is connected on a subconscious level to the rest of humanity. And just as Lilith takes on the physical appearance of Rei in the End of Eva, I think that the apparition was Lilith's soul probing into the mind of Shinji, and taking on the appearance of the vessal of her soul in the very same way. Feel free to disagree on that. Its just my interpretation.

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Jun 23, 2004 7:50 pm YEAHHH !!! :o an awesome explination !!! :P i think this is the right one !!! But also i think is a symbolic way to link the begginin and the end of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Xenogil » Sat Jun 26, 2004 5:27 pm -Knowing how big a hit Eva is, Anno and co. immediately get the chance to show us the original ending they planned in an OVA, which later became a movie as it became a bigger project. They base it off that original script for the ending that was trashed, according to the Red Cross Book.

[]:             Quote Post by Quiddity » Sat Jun 26, 2004 5:33 pm Oh, that's how End of Eva came to be? I heard that Mr. Anno received numerous threating letters from angry Eva fans, and that's why he made End of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Loves Toji » Sat Jul 03, 2004 8:30 pm Quiddity wrote: Oh, that's how End of Eva came to be? I heard that Mr. Anno received numerous threating letters from angry Eva fans, and that's why he made End of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by coff » Sat Jul 03, 2004 8:45 pm Asuka Loves Toji wrote: but! there were also screen shots of a trashed gainax facility and of the death threat letters in EoE

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Loves Toji » Sat Jul 03, 2004 9:09 pm coff wrote: Asuka Loves Toji wrote: but! there were also screen shots of a trashed gainax facility and of the death threat letters in EoE

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jul 08, 2004 2:46 am they did? the part that got me was when they had the shot of the theatre and the guy was trying to get the cameraman's attention... i heard from a lot of people that 25-26 of the original series was the original and then anno created D&R and EoE...then again...who knows???
